criteria.name: Problem Identification
criteria.description: Clearly identifies and articulates the key components of the ill-defined problem, including input and output specifications.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately describes the problem with all necessary components and constraints, using appropriate terminology.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to identify crucial components of the problem or misinterprets the problem requirements.

criteria.name: Task Specification
criteria.description: Translates the ill-defined problem into a well-defined task with clear objectives and steps to solve it.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Defines a comprehensive and clear task that outlines a logical approach to solving the problem.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides an ambiguous or incomplete task definition that lacks clarity or logical structure.

criteria.name: Algorithm Selection Consideration
criteria.description: Considers appropriate algorithms for sorting or searching based on the problem context and requirements.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Selects an optimal algorithm that is well-suited for the specific problem characteristics and justifies the choice effectively.
criteria.poor_indicator: Chooses a suboptimal or irrelevant algorithm without proper justification.

typical_errors: Misunderstanding the problem requirements leading to incorrect task framing.
Neglecting to specify input and output clearly, resulting in ambiguity.
Selecting an inappropriate algorithm based on the problem context.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Effectively identifies errors or gaps in the AI-generated output related to sorting or searching algorithms.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately points out multiple specific errors in the output with clear explanations.
criteria.poor_indicator: Identifies few or no errors, or provides vague explanations that do not demonstrate understanding.

criteria.name: Complexity Analysis
criteria.description: Evaluates the efficiency of the AI's proposed solution with respect to time and space complexity.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides a thorough analysis of the algorithm's complexity, comparing it against alternatives where relevant.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to analyze complexity or provides incorrect complexity assessments.

criteria.name: Correctness of Output
criteria.description: Assesses whether the output meets the specified requirements and constraints of the task.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Determines with confidence that the output meets all requirements and identifies any deviations.
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks significant inaccuracies in the output or incorrectly claims it meets the requirements.

typical_errors: Missing key errors in the AI output due to lack of attention to detail.
Misunderstanding algorithm complexity leading to incorrect assessments.
Assuming the AI output is correct without thorough evaluation.

criteria.name: Correction Clarity
criteria.description: Provides clear and precise commands to correct the identified errors in the AI output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Delivers specific and actionable corrections that directly address the identified errors.
criteria.poor_indicator: Gives vague or unclear commands that do not effectively resolve the issues in the output.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement
criteria.description: Demonstrates the ability to iteratively refine the AI output based on previous judgments.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Shows a clear progression of improvements in the AI output with each iteration based on feedback provided.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to make meaningful improvements in the output despite providing corrections.

criteria.name: Algorithmic Insight
criteria.description: Incorporates understanding of sorting and searching algorithms into steering commands to enhance the AI's output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Utilizes algorithmic principles effectively to guide the AI towards a more efficient solution.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides corrections without demonstrating an understanding of algorithmic principles, leading to ineffective guidance.

typical_errors: Providing corrections that do not align with the identified errors in the AI output.
Failing to refine the output in subsequent iterations, leading to stagnant results.
Incorrectly steering the AI based on misunderstandings of algorithm behavior.
